focus: Orients the reader to the research question, the scope of the literature, and the key bodies of evidence on AI's impact on labor markets.
title: Introduction: AI and the Restructuring of Labor Markets

focus: Covers foundational frameworks of task-based automation, displacement, and reinstatement of labor, drawing from seminal works on technology and employment.
title: Automation, Tasks, and Job Reinstatement: Theoretical Foundations

focus: Examines studies that measure which occupations and tasks are most exposed to AI, including classic and updated exposure rubrics for large language models.
title: Occupational Exposure to AI: Susceptibility Indices and Assessments

focus: Reviews empirical studies on AI's effects on employment levels, displacement of workers, and changes in hiring across sectors and countries.
title: Employment Displacement and Hiring: Evidence from Real-World Labor Markets

focus: Synthesizes findings from field experiments and case studies demonstrating how generative AI increases worker productivity, particularly in writing, coding, and customer service.
title: Productivity Gains from Generative AI: Field Experiments and Workplace Evidence

focus: Explores evidence on how AI-driven automation and augmentation are reshaping wage structures, skill premiums, and contributing to labor-market polarization.
title: Wage Inequality and Skill Polarization in the AI Era

focus: Focuses on changes in job quality, worker skills, the creation of new tasks, and organizational adaptations driven by AI.
title: Job Quality and Skill Transformation: New Tasks, Reskilling, and Augmentation

focus: Integrates insights across the thematic bodies, identifying where studies agree on displacement vs. reinstatement, productivity, and inequality, and highlighting divergent findings or methodological limitations.
title: Cross-Study Synthesis: Convergence, Contradictions, and Evidence Strength

focus: Summarizes the collective evidence on AI's labor-market restructuring, and outlines key gaps—such as long-run effects, firm-level dynamics, and the impact on developing economies—that future research should address.
title: Conclusions and Unresolved Research Gaps
